title: betula papyrifera var. occidentalis
intro: Betula papyrifera var. occidentalis, commonly known as the western paper birch, is a deciduous tree native to western North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions. The bark of the western paper birch has been used to treat conditions such as skin irritations, wounds, and infections, and is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing. Herbal preparations of the western paper birch can be made into a tea, salve, or tincture, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, sore throats, and skin conditions.
